assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.label: Conceptual and mental-model review
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic is primarily definitions, mental models, comparisons, or framework purpose. Scenario MCQs are more appropriate than forced code trivia.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Explain historical class-component model using a new example and justify the result.
Trace and explain lifecycle methods using a new example and justify the result.
Explain why function components are preferred using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Historical class-component model** → **Lifecycle methods** → **Why function components are preferred**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

researchSources.title: Class Components vs. Function Components
researchSources.usage: Coverage research only; no transcript wording is canonical.

sections.body: Class components use instance state, this, and lifecycle methods. They matter for legacy code but are not the preferred model for new teaching.
sections.title: Historical class-component model

sections.body: Class lifecycle methods split setup, update, and teardown by timing; Effects group setup and cleanup around one synchronization concern.
sections.title: Lifecycle methods

sections.body: Function components compose logic through Hooks, avoid this binding, and align with current React features.
sections.title: Why function components are preferred

sections.explanation: A function component expresses memory and synchronization with Hooks.
sections.title: Worked example

summary: Class Components vs. Function Components connects Historical class-component model, Lifecycle methods, Why function components are preferred.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Class Components vs. Function Components

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in class components vs. function components recur throughout later React work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **Effects and Hooks** module.
